item.title: ‘Who’s Afraid of Chinese Models?’ 摘要
item.description:
Ben Thompson argues that U.S. open-weight model makers are disadvantaged because they must follow frontier labs' terms of service, making them worse than Chinese alternatives that ignore such restrictions. He questions why distillation is considered bad, noting that LLMs themselves are distillations of internet knowledge. Thompson proposes U.S. legislation to classify training data collection as fair use and bar terms of service forbidding distillation, enabling innovation without dependence on China. Chinese models like Kimi K3 benefit from distillation, staying 6–9 months behind U.S. frontier labs, but Thompson suggests frontier labs should not complain since their own models are built on distilled public knowledge.
标签: AI, LLM, distillation, open-weight models, Chinese AI, copyright, fair use, regulation, innovation, Stratechery

item.title: Who’s Afraid of Chinese Models? 摘要
item.description:
Ben Thompson proposes a US law making training data collection fair use and barring terms of service that forbid distillation, arguing this would help US open models compete with Chinese counterparts. He suggests Alibaba's release of Qwen 3.8 Max (2.4T parameters) as open weights may have been influenced by Xi Jinping's speech encouraging open source. The article also notes Qwen 3.8 Max's impressive image generation, including a pelican riding a bicycle, with humorous reasoning trace notes.
标签: AI, open source, distillation, copyright, Qwen, Chinese AI, policy, LLM, generative AI, ethics

item.title: Reverse-engineering is cheap now 摘要
item.description:
Coding agents are reducing the cost of writing code, making it feasible to reverse-engineer and automate home devices. Previously, the effort and maintenance burden of undocumented APIs made such projects unattractive. With agents, the cost of trying and failing is lower, and the psychological weight of future maintenance is reduced, changing the ROI equation for hobbyist automation.
标签: reverse-engineering, coding-agents, ai-assisted-programming, generative-ai, ai, llms, automation, home-automation, cost-reduction, maintenance

item.title: Expensive Is Just a Brand Now 摘要
item.description:
The article challenges the traditional belief that higher price guarantees better quality. It argues that in the past, price correlated with quality due to better materials and construction, but today many premium brands manufacture in the same factories as cheap alternatives, with price reflecting brand power, marketing, and exclusivity rather than durability. The author advises consumers to judge products individually and seek reviews or 'cheap alternatives' instead of relying on price as a quality signal.
标签: consumerism, branding, quality, price, marketing, manufacturing, value, economics, retail, product durability

item.title: [Sponsor] WorkOS MCP: Manage Your Auth Platform From Any AI Agent 摘要
item.description:
WorkOS has launched an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that allows AI agents to perform administrative tasks on its auth platform, such as debugging SSO, managing users, adjusting policies, and configuring branding. Previously, these tasks required a human-driven UI. The MCP server provides agents with the same access as a dashboard login, supporting hundreds of discoverable operations. Connection is done via OAuth with scoped tokens, eliminating the need for a master API key. Users can even ask an agent to match a login page from a screenshot, enabling automation of previously manual configuration tasks.
标签: WorkOS, MCP, AI agents, authentication, SSO, devops, API, automation, configuration, OAuth
